name: My lawyer in LA used AI for my case docs—does that protect my old personal AI chats?
description: No—only lawyer-directed AI gets potential protection; disclose yours to counsel.[1]

mainEntity.name: My lawyer in LA used AI for my case docs—does that protect my old personal AI chats?

acceptedAnswer.text: No—only lawyer-directed AI gets potential protection; disclose yours to counsel.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: No, your independent prior AI chats remain unprotected; lawyer-directed AI might qualify as work product.[1] The Heppner ruling allows protection only if attorney affirmatively directs client AI use.[1] Key factors: distinction between solo vs. supervised AI; confidentiality risks with AI providers; need to verify AI accuracy.[1][2] Inform your lawyer of all past AI interactions; avoid new ones without direction; request AI use protocol from counsel.[1] Directed use strengthens protection.
